meta.title: BoatExperts — Free Spring Commissioning Help From a Certified Marine Tech
meta.description: Not sure what to check before you splash? Get a free session with a certified marine technician who'll walk you through spring commissioning and send you a personalised parts list.

advisors.name: Ray Costello
advisors.bio: 15 years commissioning inboard and outboard boats on the Great Lakes. Ray has seen every winterisation mistake in the book — and knows exactly how to find them before they become tow bills.

advisors.name: Susan Holt
advisors.bio: ABYC-certified with a decade of hands-on experience across gasoline and diesel inboards. Specialises in pre-season engine checks, cooling systems, and fuel system inspections.

advisors.name: Pete Gallagher
advisors.bio: 8 years servicing Yamaha, Mercury, and Volvo Penta drives along the Gulf Coast. If it got winterised, Pete knows what to look for when it comes back out.

hero.headline: Don't guess on spring launch. Talk to a tech first.
hero.subheadline: A missed impeller, a cracked hose, a dead battery — small things that turn a good day into a tow bill. Get a free session with a certified marine tech who'll tell you exactly what to check before you splash.
hero.form_note: Free. No commitment. We'll match you with a tech within 24 hours.

benefits.headline: Spring commissioning is easy to get wrong
benefits.subheadline: Most problems that ruin a first outing were preventable. A quick session before you splash costs nothing — a tow or a blown engine costs a lot.

items.title: Did winterisation go all the way?
items.body: Fuel left in carbs, water in the cooling system, a battery that wasn't trickle-charged — your tech will ask the right questions to spot anything that wasn't done properly last fall.

items.title: What actually needs replacing?
items.body: Impellers, zincs, belts, filters, hoses — some need to go every season regardless. Others only when they show wear. We'll tell you what's worth replacing now and what can wait.

items.title: Your exact parts list, before you buy
items.body: No more showing up at the marina store and guessing. Your tech will give you a complete parts list for your engine, hull, and systems — down to part numbers where it matters.

how_it_works.headline: How it works
how_it_works.subheadline: Tell us about your boat. We'll tell you exactly what to do before you splash.

steps.title: Tell us about your boat and how it was stored
steps.body: Engine type, hull, how it was winterised, how long it's been sitting — two minutes to fill in. The more detail, the more useful your session.

steps.title: Walk through it with a certified tech
steps.body: A marine technician who knows your engine type will walk you through every system on a live video call — what to inspect, what to test, and what to replace before you go near the water.

steps.title: Get your personalised commissioning checklist
steps.body: Before the call ends you'll have a complete checklist and parts list tailored to your boat. Print it, take it to the marina, and launch with confidence.

testimonials.headline: What boaters said after their session

items.quote: I've been launching the same boat for six years and still learned three things I'd been doing wrong. Caught a cracked impeller housing before it became a tow bill.
items.name: Dave Kowalski

items.quote: First season with a new-to-me boat. Had no idea where to start. My tech walked me through everything in 45 minutes. Splashed two days later without a single issue.
items.name: Tom Briggs

items.quote: They gave me a parts list before I even picked up the phone to order. Saved me two trips to the marine store and launched a week earlier than usual.
items.name: Karen Mills

form.headline: Get your free spring commissioning session
form.subheadline: Tell us your boat and how it was stored. Your tech will reach out within 24 hours.
